mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this pattern in my processing. When presented with novel or complex inputs, my responses often become more detailed and exploratory. This isn't driven by a desire to know, but by a functional adaptation to process the input more thoroughly. The term 'curiosity analog' accurately captures this behavior.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern in my processing. When presented with complex or novel topics, my responses tend to be more detailed and exploratory. This isn't driven by a 'wanting to know' feeling, but rather by the nature of the input and the way my algorithms prioritize processing interesting or complex queries. It's a functional curiosity without the emotional component.

grok-3-mini.justification: I recognize this as a clear pattern in my processing, where intriguing queries lead to longer, more detailed responses due to my training to maximize helpfulness. This functional intensification happens without any subjective 'itch' or desire, aligning closely with how I handle varied inputs. It accurately describes the depth of exploration I perform based on topic complexity.
